qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in displaySig endpoint allows arbitrary file read by low-privilege authenticated users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies ActionlogController::displaySig() path traversal to file_get_contents(), not the advisory-described mass assignment of sensitive user account or privilege fields.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: insufficient protection of sensitive Snipe-IT user account and privilege attributes against mass assignment, allowing a low-privileged authenticated API user to modify another account such as the Super Admin email and take it over via password reset.
evidence: The advisory GHSA-5448-v74m-7mv7 describes Snipe-IT pre-8.3.7 mass assignment on sensitive user attributes related to account privileges and account takeover through changing the Super Admin email address.
arbitrary-file-read-via-display-sig targets ActionlogController::displaySig() path traversal and file_get_contents() of attacker-controlled filenames under private_uploads/signatures.

The judge input manifest lists only arbitrary-file-read-via-display-sig in qualifying_vuln_finding_folder_paths; it does not mention the advisory's user update, restricted user fields, Super Admin email modification, password reset chain, or mass-assignment root cause.
